name: Spanish-Rice Enchiladas
recipeInstructions:
5 or 6 cups Enchilada sauce (10-12 Soft corn tortillas)
1. Place water and onion in a medium saucepan. Saute until onion softens slightly.
2. Add the spinach. Cover and steam until just tender, about 4 to 5 minutes.
3. Remove from heat. Add rice and seasonings. Mix thoroughly and set aside.
(You may substitute wine, Bragg's Amino, or vegetable broth for the water when sauteing.)
4. Preheat oven to 350°F.
5. Spread 1 cup of Enchilada sauce over the bottom of a casserole dish.
6. Spread a line of the spinach-rice mixture down the center of a tortilla.
7. Roll up and place, seam-side down, in the casserole.
8. Repeat until all ingredients are used.
9. Pour the remaining sauce over the tortillas.
10. Cover and bake for 30 minutes.
(If not following the McDougall Plan, you may top with no-fat cheddar cheese or serve with a bit of no-fat sour cream.)
